hard.next_step: We need a NDA in place before we can get the information to build the value play, we wont get that until July - however he has sent user numbers to get some indicative pricing - We have sent indictive pricing i will check in with him next week Building Value play - meeting booked next week to run through business case
hard.ais_why: A transcribed 13 May call confirms AI is in scope: Zycus was asked to price 'guided procurement with/without AI', and Phase 1 includes 'AI-guided eProcurement intake' (Merlin) with a future tail-spend autonomous-negotiation (Anna) layer. Interest is exploratory, not committed: the buyer wanted a 'non-AI fallback', and AI sits alongside core P2P modules on a 20-year-old SAP ECC landscape. Single-threaded to procurement, no AI champion or POC. Medium confidence (one to two recorded calls).

north_star_verdict.headline: Healthy, engaged deal but the 31 Dec close cannot hold: buyer plans a July CapEx approval then a formal multi-vendor RFP in October, with implementation set for 2027; still single-threaded to Alessandro with the Decision Maker and budget owner unengaged.
north_star_verdict.math: 178 days to close (2026-12-31 vs today 2026-07-06). Created 2026-02-20, qualified 2026-05-08, moved Qualified -> Formal Evaluation 2026-06-04. Amount stepped 150k -> 350k on 2026-06-04 as Anna and optional source-to-pay costing were added. Buyer's own timeline (Alessandro, 2026-06-04): IT submits CapEx budget in July, approval by end-July, RFP drafted Aug/Sept, released October, run Nov/Dec, implementation for 2027. That sequence cannot complete a signature by 31 Dec 2026, so the close date is a placeholder that will slip into 2027. Deal is genuinely progressing and buyer engaged (last two-way exchange 2026-07-02, Alessandro: 'meeting went well and we are now in the process of finalising the business case'), so the sole real problem is the date, not momentum. Forecast Pipeline is honest for Formal Evaluation.
evidence: Last genuine two-way exchange 2026-07-02: Alessandro Barcellona confirmed 'Meeting went well and we are now in the process of finalising the business case' and agreed a follow-up call for Tuesday next week (SF task, 2026-07-02).

Buyer-owned timeline pushes the real decision past 31 Dec: 'in July, IT will submit the budget for the Capex... if approved, we'll know by end of July... September to December is probably when we do the RFP... to prepare for 2027' (Alessandro, Avoma 2026-06-04).

Single-threaded to Alessandro Barcellona (Head of Procurement Excellence, Evaluator) across every call; Jennifer Parise (Director of Procurement UKI, the Decision Maker and Alessandro's manager) has never joined a call (SF OpportunityContactRole; Avoma 2026-05-13, 2026-06-04).
No named Economic Buyer engaged: 'IT will submit the budget' and the budget-conscious UK MD (ex-Turkey) sponsors the spend, but neither has spoken to Zycus (Alessandro, Avoma 2026-05-13, 2026-06-04).
Zycus is the buyer-stated front-runner but only informally pre-RFP: 'After Vienna, you become top one for me' (Alessandro, Avoma 2026-05-13); the field opens up again at the formal October RFP.

Indicative pricing landed (£125,000-£140,000 annual SaaS + £180,000 implementation) and the 22 June Chiswick onsite ran, so commercials are in motion at Formal Evaluation (SF email 2026-06-23; SF event 2026-06-22).

metrics.narrative: The value case is quantified and buyer-validated. On the 2026-06-04 value-engineering call Alessandro Barcellona put UK indirect spend at roughly £300M ('for GB it should be around 300 millions in total pounds') and agreed to model ~£30M (10% of the indirect tail) through Anna, the autonomous-negotiation agent, at a conservative 5% saving: 'if you're looking at 5% of 30,000,000, then you're gonna pay this back 5 to 6 times' the platform cost. Indicative commercials are on the table: £125,000-£140,000 annual SaaS plus a one-off £180,000 implementation (SF email, Claire Hudson, 2026-06-23). The UK MD's directive frames the metric that matters: 'be super mindful of budget. If the budget is higher, you need to demonstrate the value' (Alessandro quoting the MD, Avoma 2026-05-13). The only open item is the completed value questionnaire that Zycus value engineers will turn into the final business case, which Alessandro said on 2026-07-02 is now being finalised.
sources: Avoma value-engineering call 2026-06-04, Alessandro Barcellona: UK indirect spend ~£300M, ~£30M through Anna at 5% = 5-6x platform payback
SF email 2026-06-23, Claire Hudson: indicative pricing £125k-£140k SaaS + £180k implementation
Avoma discovery 2026-05-13, Alessandro (quoting UK MD): 'if the budget is higher, you need to demonstrate the value'
SF task 2026-07-02, Alessandro: 'we are now in the process of finalising the business case'

economic_buyer.narrative: The budget authority is described but not named or engaged. Alessandro was explicit on 2026-06-04: 'in July, IT will submit the budget for the Capex... if it is approved, which we'll probably know by the end of July.' The commercial tone-setter is the UK Managing Director (who came from Turkey and initiated the SAP-enhancement project), described as 'always be super mindful of budget' (Avoma 2026-05-13) - so the spend is sponsored by the MD and formally submitted by an unnamed IT leader. Jennifer Parise (Director of Procurement UKI) is Alessandro's manager and the Salesforce Decision Maker; Alessandro said 'I'm thinking about my manager... Jennifer. We've met, we've exchanged mails' (Avoma 2026-06-04) but she has not engaged Zycus directly. So the economic buyer sits with IT/Finance CapEx approval under the MD, and Zycus has no line to any of them - the move is to get Alessandro to bring the CapEx submitter and Jennifer into the business-case review before the July submission.
sources: Avoma 2026-06-04, Alessandro: 'in July, IT will submit the budget for the Capex... approved by end of July'
Avoma 2026-05-13, Alessandro quoting UK MD: budget-conscious sponsor who started the project
SF OpportunityContactRole: Jennifer Parise, Director of Procurement UKI, Decision Maker (no call attendance)

decision_criteria.narrative: The evaluation criteria are clear from the two discovery calls. Pladis wants a modern, fit-for-purpose P2P platform to replace a 20-year-old SAP ECC that has 'nothing' for P2P, prioritising: (1) digital supplier collaboration and PO/scheduling-agreement management (the 'supply chain collaboration' capability Alessandro knew from Ariba), (2) guided/intake procurement with an 'Amazon-type experience', and (3) invoicing - with Merlin's agentic AI (Anna autonomous negotiation for sub-£5k tail) as the differentiator that impressed Alessandro at the Vienna event: 'what I've seen in Vienna was quite impressive in terms of Merlin' (Avoma 2026-05-13). A hard criterion is budget flexibility: Alessandro asked for a 'step two that doesn't have AI but is a little bit cheaper' as a fallback, so cost-to-value must survive scrutiny. Sourcing, contracting CLM and risk are explicitly out of scope for Phase 1 because they sit with the global source-to-contract team. These criteria will be formalised in the October RFP.
sources: Avoma 2026-05-13, Alessandro: modern platform vs 20-yr SAP ECC, supplier collaboration, guided procurement, Merlin impressed at Vienna, non-AI fallback wanted
Avoma 2026-06-04, Alessandro: P2P scope = supplier management, eProc, intake, invoicing; sourcing/contracting/risk out of scope

decision_process.narrative: Alessandro mapped the process himself on 2026-06-04: get indicative pricing (done), Zycus value engineers help build the business case (in progress), then 'in July, IT will submit the budget for the Capex', approval expected 'by the end of July', August left out for holiday, then 'September to December is probably when we do the RFP' with release 'probably October', to 'prepare for 2027'. This is a phased land-and-expand: Phase 1 UK P2P as the entry point, then 'go to the full source-to-pay' - Anthony Gray's 'Trojan horse' framing that Alessandro accepted. Regionally Alessandro owns P2P and reports to Jennifer Parise (Director of Procurement UKI); the global procurement-excellence COE owns source-to-contract and would be pulled in for the wider expansion. Approval gates on an internal CapEx submission through IT under the budget-conscious UK MD, so the process risk is that the go/no-go is made by people Zycus has never met.
sources: Avoma 2026-06-04, Alessandro: pricing -> business case -> July IT CapEx submission -> end-July approval -> Oct RFP -> 2027 implementation
Avoma 2026-05-13, Alessandro/Anthony Gray: phased P2P-first 'Trojan horse' into full source-to-pay; global COE owns S2C

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are early but two signals are already live. First, an NDA is a gating step before Pladis shares the detailed value-play data: 'based on our internal policy, we will have to put an NDA in place... we wont get that until July' (Alessandro, SF email 2026-06-19; echoed in Next Step). Second, the formal buying vehicle is a post-budget RFP that Alessandro intends to release in October, so the real paper process (MSA/SOW/redlines) is downstream of that competitive round. On implementation, Alessandro probed whether a separate implementation partner is needed; Claire confirmed the £180k implementation is a Zycus cost that includes Zycus's delivery partners (SF emails 2026-06-23). No MSA, SOW, legal, InfoSec or procurement-onboarding activity has started yet - which is normal for Formal Evaluation. Visibility improves once the NDA is signed and the RFP structure is shared.
sources: SF email 2026-06-19, Alessandro: 'based on our internal policy, we will have to put an NDA in place... wont get that until July'
Avoma 2026-06-04 & Next Step: formal RFP to be released October post budget approval
SF emails 2026-06-23, Claire Hudson: £180k implementation is a Zycus cost including delivery partners

identify_pain.narrative: The operational pain is concrete and owner-attributed. Pladis UK runs a 20-year-old SAP ECC as its ERP with 'nothing' purpose-built for P2P: 'for p two p, there is nothing. So ERP is where things happen' (Alessandro, Avoma 2026-05-13). Turkey (the largest region) uses an ECC module (loosely 'SAP SRM') for POs, scheduling agreements and supplier communication, but the assessment concluded it 'in 3 years would be no longer maintained' and 'we want to explore more modern solutions'. The pain owner is Alessandro Barcellona, who owns regional procurement operations (PO raising, scheduling agreements, purchasing cards), driven by a UK MD who wants a fit-for-purpose modern platform rather than sticking with SAP by default. The urgency is real but budget-gated: this P2P need 'came out as urgent' from the UK & Ireland procurement leadership Steerco, ahead of the slower global source-to-contract tender.
sources: Avoma 2026-05-13, Alessandro: 20-yr SAP ECC, no P2P functionality, Turkey's SRM module to be deprecated, MD wants modern fit-for-purpose platform
Avoma 2026-05-13, Alessandro: P2P need 'came out as urgent' from UK&I procurement leadership Steerco

champion.narrative: Alessandro Barcellona (Head of Procurement Excellence UKIRL, Evaluator) is a developing, actively advocating champion. Evidence of advocacy: he rated Zycus 'top one' after the Vienna event ('After Vienna, you become top one for me', Avoma 2026-05-13), volunteered UK volumes and the £300M indirect-spend figure, asked for indicative pricing ahead of his own 1:1 with his manager ('I have my 121 with my manager today at 11.00. Any chance you can send me the indicative prices before that?', SF email 2026-06-23), hosted Zycus onsite at Chiswick on 2026-06-22, and is co-building the business case with Zycus value engineers. What is still missing is the reason he is 'developing' not 'strong': he has not yet brought Jennifer Parise (his manager, the Decision Maker) or the IT CapEx/UK-MD budget owner into a Zycus conversation, so his sponsorship is real but not yet backed by power. He remains engaged as of 2026-07-02 ('meeting went well').
sources: Avoma 2026-05-13, Alessandro: 'After Vienna, you become top one for me'
SF email 2026-06-23, Alessandro: requested indicative pricing before his manager 1:1
SF event 2026-06-22: Alessandro hosted Zycus onsite at Chiswick Park
SF OpportunityContactRole: Alessandro Evaluator; Jennifer Parise DM has not engaged

competition.narrative: This is currently a sole-source, pre-RFP position rather than a live bake-off. The only named alternative is the incumbent SAP ECC (the 20-year-old ERP with no real P2P layer), which the buyer is actively trying to move off: 'we don't want just to stick to SAP because we have SAP' (Alessandro, Avoma 2026-05-13) - a negative-sentiment incumbent Zycus is displacing, not a preferred rival. Their existing source-to-contract savings tool 'Jager' (Jaggaer) sits in the adjacent global S2C space with a longer, separate tender timeline and is not in this P2P scope. No competing P2P vendor has been named. The genuine competitive risk is future and structural: Alessandro will run a formal, multi-vendor RFP in October, at which point rivals (and SAP's incumbency/lower-perceived-risk) re-enter. Zycus's wedge is the modern platform plus Merlin/Anna agentic AI that impressed Alessandro at Vienna; the job before October is to convert his informal 'top one' preference into RFP criteria that favour agentic AI.
sources: Avoma 2026-05-13, Alessandro: buyer moving off 20-yr SAP ECC; 'Jager' (Jaggaer) is their S2C savings tool on a separate global timeline
Avoma 2026-06-04, Alessandro: formal RFP to be released October (multi-vendor)
Avoma 2026-05-13, Alessandro: Merlin impressed him at Vienna ('top one')

deal_movement.summary: Deal has strengthened since the last sweep: the value questionnaire that was overdue is resolved, the business case is being finalised, and the buyer confirmed a productive onsite. Last real two-way touch 2026-07-02 (Alessandro). Amount stepped up 150k->350k on 2026-06-04 as Anna and optional source-to-pay scope were added, and stage advanced Qualified->Formal Evaluation the same day.

items.change: Alessandro confirmed the 22 June onsite 'went well' and the business case is being finalised; agreed a follow-up call for the following Tuesday
In-person value session held at Pladis, Chiswick Park Building 3
Indicative pricing issued: £125,000-£140,000 annual SaaS + £180,000 implementation (Zycus cost, partners included)
Alessandro flagged an NDA is required (internal policy) before detailed value-play data can be shared, expected July
Amount raised 150,000 -> 350,000 and stage advanced Qualified -> Formal Evaluation as Anna and optional S2P costing entered scope
Value-engineering scope-and-ROI session: ~£300M UK indirect spend, ~£30M through Anna at 5% = 5-6x payback

competitive_position.summary: Zycus is the informal front-runner pre-RFP, not in a live competitive fight. The single most relevant competitor today is the incumbent SAP ECC, which the buyer is explicitly trying to replace (negative sentiment, being displaced) - so incumbency is a low current threat. 'Jager' (Jaggaer) is their existing source-to-contract savings tool on a separate, slower global timeline, not a P2P competitor here. No P2P rival vendor has been named. The real competitive risk is future and structural: Alessandro's formal multi-vendor RFP in October reopens the field, where SAP's familiarity and lower-perceived-deployment-risk re-enter. Zycus's wedge is the modern platform plus Merlin/Anna agentic AI that made it 'top one' after Vienna (Alessandro, 2026-05-13).

competitors.name: SAP ECC (incumbent)
competitors.quote: We have SAP as ERP... for p two p, there is nothing. It's an ECC model from 20 years ago. We are not going to put something that in 3 years would be no longer maintained. We want to explore more modern solutions. We don't want just to stick to SAP.
competitors.how_we_win: Position the modern platform plus Merlin/Anna agentic AI as the fit-for-purpose replacement SAP ECC cannot offer; make the 20-year maintenance risk the do-nothing cost.

competitors.name: Jaggaer (Jager, S2C incumbent)
competitors.quote: Jager is a source to contract and savings recording... it's not really wide to utilize from the regions. There is a global led project in due course to do a tender, but the timing is quite longer compared to our p two p project.
competitors.how_we_win: Stay focused on winning P2P Phase 1 now; use a delivered P2P success story as the wedge into the later global source-to-contract tender where Jaggaer sits.

competitors.name: Formal RFP field (October, multi-vendor)
competitors.quote: September to December is probably when we do the RFP... release the RFP in September or October... I would say probably October.
competitors.how_we_win: Shape the RFP with the P2P/agentic-AI primer before October so the criteria favour Merlin/Anna, converting the informal 'top one' lead into a structural advantage.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Strong fit against Pladis's stated P2P needs: modern supplier collaboration, guided/intake procurement and agentic AI on the tail. The exposed edge is budget-approval risk and SAP ECC integration complexity, both acknowledged by the buyer.

items.criterion: Modern, fit-for-purpose P2P platform to replace 20-yr SAP ECC
items.quote: we want to explore more modern solutions... it's what we need as a business, what's fit for purpose

if for any reason budget become a problem, you have step two that doesn't have AI but is a little bit cheaper and might be within the budget

items.deliverable: Confirm with the solution team whether iSource Lite is required to enable Anna autonomous negotiation

best_practice_check.summary: A healthy, buyer-led deal whose win rests on three levers before the October RFP: multi-thread to power, lock the ROI as the CapEx justification, and shape the RFP around agentic AI.
flags: Multi-thread now: get Alessandro to bring Jennifer Parise (Director of Procurement UKI, Decision Maker) and the IT CapEx submitter into the business-case review before the July budget decision.
Convert the ROI into the CapEx justification: land the finalised £300M-spend / £1.5M-Anna-savings model as the artifact Alessandro takes to IT and the budget-conscious UK MD.
Pre-wire the October RFP: use the P2P and agentic-AI primer to shape the criteria toward Merlin/Anna so Zycus's informal 'top one' lead survives the multi-vendor round.

items.name: Alessandro Barcellona
items.title: Head of Procurement Excellence UKIRL
items.sentiment: Positive and active: rated Zycus 'top one' after Vienna, drives the business case, hosted the Chiswick onsite, and volunteered spend/volume data.
items.risk: Single point of contact. He has not yet brought his manager (the Decision Maker) or the IT/UK-MD budget owner into a Zycus conversation, so his advocacy is not yet backed by power ahead of the October RFP.

items.name: Jennifer Parise
items.title: Director of Procurement UKI
items.sentiment: Neutral / unengaged: holds the Decision Maker contact role and is Alessandro's manager, but has not attended any call.
items.risk: Unengaged Decision Maker 178 days before close. Without her validation before the October RFP, her evaluation lens and competitive exposure are unknown.
items.source: SF OpportunityContactRole (Decision Maker); Avoma 2026-06-04 (Alessandro: 'my manager... Jennifer')

items.title: IT CapEx submission owner (unnamed)
items.risk: The July CapEx submission and end-July approval run through an unnamed IT leader and the budget-conscious UK Managing Director, neither of whom Zycus has met. The go/no-go is being made for an audience Zycus cannot yet influence.
items.source: Avoma 2026-06-04, Alessandro: 'IT will submit the budget for the Capex'; Avoma 2026-05-13: UK MD is the budget-conscious sponsor

champion_strength.summary: Alessandro Barcellona (Head of Procurement Excellence UKIRL, Evaluator) is a genuine, active advocate: he rated Zycus 'top one' after Vienna, co-builds the business case, requested pricing ahead of his manager 1:1, and hosted Zycus onsite on 2026-06-22. He is 'developing' rather than 'strong' because he has not yet brought the Decision Maker (Jennifer Parise, his manager) or the IT/UK-MD budget owner into a Zycus conversation, so his sponsorship is not yet backed by power. Engaged as recently as 2026-07-02.
champion_strength.champion: Alessandro Barcellona (Head of Procurement Excellence UKIRL)
champion_strength.source: SF OpportunityContactRole (Evaluator); Avoma 2026-05-13 & 2026-06-04; SF email 2026-06-23; SF task 2026-07-02

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Well positioned on AI narrative: Merlin guided procurement and the Anna autonomous-negotiation agent are central to the buyer's interest and the quantified ROI (~£30M tail through Anna, 5-6x payback). The nuance is that Alessandro wants a non-AI fallback for budget safety, so AI is the differentiator but must not become the affordability blocker.
ai_positioning_strength.score: 5 (Moderate) - fairly rated; calls confirm real AI interest but exploratory, budget-gated, and single-threaded with no AI champion above Alessandro.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS 5.0 / Moderate is well-calibrated. The 13 May and 4 Jun calls confirm genuine appetite for Merlin guided procurement and Anna autonomous negotiation (impressed at Vienna, built into the ROI), but the buyer explicitly wants a cheaper non-AI fallback and there is no AI-specific champion or POC, so it is curiosity with a budget guardrail rather than committed AI demand.

items.detail: Single-threaded to Alessandro; the Decision Maker (Jennifer Parise) and the IT/UK-MD budget owner are unengaged, so the July CapEx go/no-go is made by people Zycus has never met.
Close date 31 Dec 2026 is not credible: RFP releases October, runs Nov/Dec, implementation targeted for 2027; the date will slip into 2027.
Budget-conscious UK MD directive - 'if the budget is higher, you need to demonstrate the value' - plus an affordable non-AI fallback request mean the ROI must be airtight or the AI-led scope is cut.

Do-nothing / delay risk: approval gates on an internal CapEx cycle and a formal RFP; if the July budget slips, the whole timeline drifts to 2027 with no active competitor - a no-decision risk more than a loss risk.

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement is live and warming: buyer volunteered spend data, requested pricing ahead of a manager 1:1, hosted the onsite, and confirmed on 2026-07-02 the business case is being finalised. Momentum is genuine but bounded to Phase 1 P2P and gated on a July internal budget cycle.
items: Buyer-initiated: Alessandro asked for indicative prices before his 2026-06-23 manager 1:1 (pull, not push).
Concrete buyer-owned dates: July CapEx submission, end-July approval, October RFP, 2027 implementation.
Commercials in motion: indicative pricing issued and onsite value session held.

customer_preference.evidence: Alessandro Barcellona: 'After Vienna, you become top one for me in terms of somebody to deliver' (Avoma 2026-05-13); he is co-building the business case with Zycus value engineers and asked for Zycus-specific commercials ahead of his manager 1:1.

business_case.evidence: Quantified with the buyer: ~£300M UK indirect spend, ~£30M through Anna at 5% saving = 5-6x platform payback; indicative £125k-£140k SaaS + £180k implementation (Avoma 2026-06-04; SF email 2026-06-23).
